page: Amazon Web Services c8i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-threaded performance, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression rates placing in the top 10% of tested instances. Multi-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls into the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show 640.93 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and approximately 59.81 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The hardware also demonstrates strong text generation performance for small language models, achieving 291.25 tokens per second.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning storage-reliant applications must utilize network-attached storage, and heavy parallel graphics processing is not supported. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps. Given the high single-core performance and dedicated CPU allocation, the c8i.2xlarge is suited for compute-bound workloads, single-threaded data processing, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory, a 480 MB L3 cache, and dedicated CPU allocation, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX). Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores in the top 10%. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics align with mid-tier averages. Lacking local storage, the instance relies on network storage over a 4 Gbps baseline network interface. This profile makes the c8i.2xlarge highly efficient for compute-intensive tasks, single-threaded processing, software compilation, and small-scale model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized instance with an Intel Xeon processor, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An x86 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, DDR5 memory, and exceptional single-threaded performance for compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with a 480 MB L3 cache
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix operations
No local storage or GPU, relying on network-attached storage and a 4 Gbps baseline network
Best suited for software compilation, single-threaded data processing, and light inference
